title: Route memory report through portable primitives

execplan_profile.projection_rule: canonical_core is authoritative for intent, scope, next action, proof, continuation, and closeout; legacy fields remain compatibility projections.

canonical_core.requested_outcome: Route agentic-memory report --format json non-verbose through portable operation primitives instead of a generated memory-runtime helper.
canonical_core.hard_constraints: Do not move verbose/text memory report behavior in this slice. Do not copy package-specific runtime mechanics into command-generation. Preserve generated CLI conformance and explicit fallback debt accounting.
canonical_core.agent_may_decide: Add small generic payload-template support if needed, update the memory.report operation contract, regenerate target outputs, and tighten static proof.
canonical_core.escalate_when: The compact JSON report requires policy beyond manifest table counts, target-root resolution, generic payload assembly, or output emission.
canonical_core.next_action: Add generic payload-template selectors, move memory.report compact JSON to portable IR, regenerate outputs, and validate.
proof_expectations: Primitive executor tests cover any new generic template support.
Generated package checks prove the memory report compact JSON path uses portable primitives and the runtime facade keeps only verbose/text fallback.
Python, node, and docker conformance for generated command packages remain green.
completion_criteria: Non-verbose JSON memory report is produced by path.target_root.resolve, toml.table.counts, payload.assemble, and output.emit.
Generated memory_runtime no longer owns _tiny_memory_report_payload_from_toml_table_counts or a memory.report compact JSON fast path.
Verbose/text memory.report.load remains explicit package-domain fallback debt.
Planning artefacts record completion and the larger #892 lane remains open unless the full epic intent is proven.
